preview_text:
The model favors Senegal at 61%, a decisive edge over Iraq's 12% win probability. The market consensus is substantially more confident in a Senegal victory, placing it at 78%—a gap of 17 percentage points. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the sportsbook view, suggesting broad agreement among commercial forecasters.

The model sees meaningful uncertainty in the outcome. A draw carries 27% probability, making it nearly as likely as an Iraq win when the two are combined. This spread indicates a competitive match where Senegal is favored but not overwhelmingly so. The model assigns roughly one-in-five odds to an upset, reflecting material parity beyond the headline win gap.

The divergence between model and market is substantial. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both compress Iraq's chances to 7–8% and the draw to 13–14%, implying greater confidence in a Senegal victory than the Dixon-Coles framework suggests. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects true match uncertainty; the gap warrants noting that commercial and statistical expectations diverge meaningfully here.
